result:
## Report

**Root cause:** Weak tables (`setmetatable(t, {__mode = 'kv'})`) were unimplemented — `LuaTable::set_metatable` in `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:167` ignored the metatable's `__mode`, and `LuaTable::get`/`next_pair` never swept weakly-only-held entries. The very first thing closure.lua does after `print "testing closures"` is `local x = {[1] = {}}; setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'}); while x[1] do A = A+1 end`, which is intended to spin until the GC reclaims x[1]. Without weak-table support the loop runs forever and the 30 s harness timer kills the process silently — exactly the "stops after testing closures" signature.

**Fix:** Added a `weak_mode: Cell<u8>` flag on the value.rs `LuaTable`, populated by `set_metatable` (`extract_weak_mode` scans the new metatable for a `__mode` string with `'k'`/`'v'`). `LuaTable::get` and `next_pair` now check `Rc::strong_count == 1` on weakly-tagged entries (the table itself is the only holder ⇒ unreachable except via the weak slot) and drop them, returning `Nil`.

**State after fix:** Build is green. With the weak-table fix, the script now advances through the entire first GC section, the equality block, and the closing `do` block — five sections further than before. A second, distinct hang surfaces afterward at the bytecode for `for i=1,10 do … if i==3 then break end end` (the `break` path through `OP_CLOSE`/`close_upval` when the loop body is short enough), but that is a separate codegen/upvalue bug unrelated to the weak-table root cause and is out of budget for this pass.

tool_input.command: cp /tmp/t.lua reference/lua-c/testes/_tmp_t.lua && ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/_tmp_t.lua 2>&1 | tail -30 && cat harness/impl/official/_tmp_t.out | tail -30
rm reference/lua-c/testes/_tmp_t.lua harness/impl/official/_tmp_t.* 2>/dev/null; cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | tail -3
